calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Apportioning Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dobrov, for and with petitioner Kent Guthrie; Laura Woodward, in pro per.

The Court asked if anyone had something to add to their papers now that Ms. Woodward had provided the Court with her time sheets. Mr. Dobrov responded that he had put in more time beyond that stated in the petition. Ms. Woodward stated that her request for fees was unopposed and that she had nothing to add. The matter was taken under submission. The Court directed Mr. Dobrov to provide the Court with his proposed order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

Petition For Order Apportioning Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Continued To Sep-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laura Woodward; Michael Dobrov for petitioner Kent Guthrie.

Ms. Woodward stated that she needed to file a declaration regarding extraordinary fees, and that she would have the declaration on file by the end of the week. The Court continued the matter to September 13, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, for final arguments, and directed that any response to Ms. Woodward's declaration be filed by August 26, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

Petition For Order Apportioning Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Continued To Aug-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dobrov for and with (executor) Kent Guthrie. Laura Woodward, former attorney for executor in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that we are here on the third amended final petition as well a petition for apportionment of fees, that in regards to the first petition there are some issues with the information schedules and that the Court is inclined to accept but not approve the accounting. Mr. Dobrov responded that this was okay, that neither he or the CPA understood the last request from the examiner and that they made calls to the examiner with no response. Ms. Woodward stated that she did not receive the petition for apportionment until 7/13/16, that she has not had time to review it, that declarations were filed on 7/11/16 & 7/12/16, that on 7/12/16 she and Mr. Dobrov had a discussion regarding settlement, that she does not know what work he has done and requests that the Court provide her with copies of the examiner's notes. The Court asked Mr. Dobrov if he had any objections to a continuance and if there is any reason that he cannot share documents with Ms. Woodward. Mr. Dobrov stated that the examiner's notes are irrelevant after she was no longer Mr. Guthrie's attorney, that his computer crashed and he has no electronic files and that he will not go through the files looking for paper copies. The Court stated that it will not regenerate notes. Ms. Woodward stated that it is relevant because counsel is comparing hours. The Court requested that the parties talk it out between themselves, stated that the petition is separate and will be continued and asked if it makes sense to continue the petition for final distribution. Mr. Dobrov requested that it not be continued stating that he had asked that everything be approved/accepted except the fees. Ms. Woodward stated that she will add fees to her objection and that Mr. Dobrov has a reserve of $20,000.00 that can be used to pay her. The Court stated that it will need an amended order spelling out the reserve, that it covers the requested fees even if they are not ultimately granted, that it is not clear that Ms. Woodward has the upside, that 50/50 is generous under the circumstances and that it hopes the parties can talk. The Court granted the petition for final distribution (subject to review of the order). The Petition for Order Apportioning Attorney's Fees was continued to August 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Objections to the petition are to be filed by August 10, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Continued To Apr-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dobrov for and with Kent Guthrie. Laura Woodward (former attorney for Kent Guthrie).

The Court stated that we are here on a pending amended accounting, that issues have been raised by the examiner and now Ms. Woodward has filed a declaration asserting entitlement to attorney's fees. Mr. Dobrov stated that they can deal with the attorney's fees in a separate petition and that he will talk to Ms. Woodward. The Court stated that issues were sent to Mr. Dobrov from the examiner, that a verification is needed, that the examiner does not have a complet copy of the 2nd supplement and that the order is not in the appropriate form. Mr. Dobrov stated that he did not receive notes from the examiner and that the issue with the supplement is a problem with e-filing. The Court read the examiner's notes, stated that the issues need to be corrected, that counsel should talk to each other regarding Ms. Woodward's fees, that a petition will be needed and that the estate cannot close until then. Mr. Dobrov asked if they could get authorization for a preliminary distribution. The Court stated that it could not be authorized without a petition, that they should be close to the end of this, that they would be at the end if things had been done correctly, that they will need to notice and that it is not telling him what to do regarding Ms. Woodward's fees. Mr. Dobrov asked if distribution could be separated from the issue of fees. The court stated that he can make the request. The accounting petition was continued to April 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to address the problems with the current petition.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laura Woodward for Kent Guthrie.

Ms. Woodward pointed out that the Children of the Decedent's half Sibling are not beneficiaries of the will, stated that she had been a Friend and Attorney of Charles for the last 20 years and that there was no connection with half Brother. Ms. Woodward also stated that addresses were received from Friends and named Beneficiaries, and explained efforts made in locating the Heirs (search of obituaries and white pages on the internet). The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
